title: Global tech slump drags down stock markets
summary: Wall Street, European, and Asian indices record heavy losses led by the semiconductor sector, as fears grow about the sustainability of AI investment.

title: Regulation and AI bubble set the agenda for markets
summary: Trump administration pressures Meta to review its AI models, government shuts down an AI model, and Oracle cuts 21,000 jobs to focus on AI, while debate grows over sector overvaluation.

title: SpaceX issues $25 billion in bonds and its market value plummets
summary: The aerospace company places its first bond issuance, but its market capitalization drops sharply amid doubts about its valuation.

title: Private credit funds face tensions over redemption restrictions
summary: Apollo's flagship fund satisfies less than 30% of redemption requests in the second quarter, as private equity rethinks its model amid liquidity pressures.

title: Samsung announces a 90 trillion won share buyback program
summary: The South Korean giant plans the largest buyback in its history, causing a strong rebound in its shares after the recent sector decline.

title: Nike faces more challenges after CFO change, says Evercore
summary: The analysis firm warns that the company faces additional difficulties in a context of executive transformation.

title: Coinbase poised to benefit from US cryptocurrency reform
summary: Bank of America says the exchange platform could take advantage of a clearer regulatory framework in the country.

title: Alphabet replaces Verizon in the Dow Jones
summary: The stock index adds Google's parent company and removes the telephone operator, reflecting technological change.

title: South Korea boosts chip investment as AI boom overheats real estate market
summary: The government negotiates new semiconductor investments with Samsung and SK Hynix, while citizens reinvest stock market gains in properties.

title: Closure of the Strait of Hormuz leaves nearly 1,200 ships stranded with $125 billion in goods
summary: Oman establishes an alternative corridor for tankers, as the maritime crisis threatens global crude trade.

title: European Parliament gives green light to digital euro
summary: The European Parliament approves the creation of the ECB's digital currency, although banks fear unfair competition.

title: ECB warns inflation will remain high for longer
summary: Senior European central bank officials anticipate that inflation will persist, which could delay rate cuts.

title: Interest rate hikes weigh on US stocks
summary: Monetary tightening and the expectation of higher rates affect equities, especially the technology sector.

title: Sanrio reports record results but shares fall on unmet expectations
summary: The Japanese entertainment company reports historic profits, but the market expected more, causing a stock decline.

title: Citi upgrades Macerich recommendation and sees potential in restructuring
summary: The bank raises the real estate company's rating to buy, highlighting its transformation plan as a catalyst.

title: SK Hynix prepares to file for ADR offering in the United States
summary: The South Korean chipmaker takes steps to list on the US stock exchange, according to regulatory filings.

title: Ibex 35 holds near 19,500 points amid uncertainty
summary: The Spanish index trades with ups and downs, preserving most of its recent gains despite the global tech collapse.

title: US bond market wobbles amid uncertainty over Fed
summary: The record spread in fixed-income markets reflects concern about monetary policy and sovereign debt.

title: Semiconductor manufacturers suffer double-digit stock declines
summary: Tokyo Electron, Sandisk, and Micron lead sector losses amid the collapse of the SOX index.

title: US boosts nuclear energy and green steel with new investments
summary: Washington grants a $15 billion loan to build nuclear reactors, while the steel industry explores low-carbon alternatives.
